name: osTicket Helpdesk API
what_it_does: Open-source support ticketing system with REST API for creating and managing support tickets — one of the most widely deployed self-hosted helpdesks globally, with email piping, web forms, and API for ticket creation and retrieval.
use_cases: Creating support tickets programmatically from external systems or monitoring alerts
Retrieving ticket status and customer information for reporting agents
Integrating osTicket with CRM or ERP systems via API for unified customer view
Automating ticket creation from CI/CD pipelines for deployment incidents
Building custom intake forms that create osTicket tickets via API
not_for: Modern multi-channel support with chat and social media (use Zendesk or Freshdesk)
Advanced ITSM workflows with change management (use ServiceNow or Jira SM)
Teams needing cloud-hosted managed service without self-hosting capacity
best_when: A team needs a proven, simple, open-source ticket management system with API access and zero per-agent cost, already widely deployed in IT and support organizations.
avoid_when: You need modern UX, real-time chat support, or multi-channel social media integration.

auth.notes: API key created in osTicket admin panel. Passed as X-API-Key header. IP whitelist required — API only accessible from configured IPs. No OAuth — simple key-based auth with IP restriction.

pricing.free_tier_limits: GPL v2 open source — completely free; no limits
pricing.notes: GPL v2 — completely free to self-host. One of the most widely deployed helpdesks globally. Runs on PHP/MySQL — easily hosted on cheap shared hosting. Large community and plugin ecosystem.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; limited by server hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Very limited API — only ticket creation and retrieval endpoints. Documentation sparse and primarily in GitHub readme. IP whitelist required adds operational complexity. No webhook support — polling required for status updates.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ticket creation creates new tickets — no deduplication. Read operations are idempotent. No idempotency key support in API.
known_agent_gotchas: IP whitelist REQUIRED — agents must call from known IPs or API calls fail
Very limited API surface — many operations only available via web UI
No webhook support — agents must poll for ticket status changes
Documentation minimal — read source code for complete API behavior

security_score_components.security_notes: GPL v2 open source. TLS depends on server/reverse proxy configuration. API key + IP whitelist provides reasonable security. Self-hosted gives full data control. Long-standing product with security patches maintained.
